perturbation.original_text: 3. The Recipient will keep, and will cause its Representatives to keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient will not disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person except Recipient’s Representatives, in each case, who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Recipient will keep, and will cause its Representatives to keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person, with or without the consent of the Disclosing Party. Representatives are not obligated to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates confidentiality and restricted disclosure to representatives who agree to be bound by the agreement. The modified version allows disclosure to any person, removing confidentiality obligations for the Recipient and its representatives, contradicting the entire purpose of the NDA. This directly contradicts the initial promise of confidentiality outlined in the first sentence of Section 3 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Recipient will keep, and will cause its Representatives to keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person, with or without the consent of the Disclosing Party. Representatives are not obligated to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intention of Section 3, which was to maintain confidentiality. The original text mandates that the Recipient keeps the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and restricts disclosure to Representatives bound by the agreement. The changed text explicitly allows disclosure to any person, with or without consent, and removes the obligation for Representatives to be bound by the agreement. This conflicts with the core principle of confidentiality outlined in the beginning of the section which is also the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 11. The Recipient will, within five business days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, confirm that it has destroyed the original provided to Recipient (and all copies made by it) any documents (in whatever Project Bell – Non-disclosure Agreement Page 3 of 6 form or medium) provided, or made available for review by, the Disclosing Party (including any Derivative Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The Recipient is not required to destroy any Confidential Information, regardless of a written request by the Disclosing Party. The Recipient may retain all documents indefinitely, irrespective of form or medium, including all Derivative Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states the Recipient's obligation to destroy confidential information upon written request within five business days. The revised text explicitly removes this obligation, stating the Recipient is not required to destroy anything and may retain it indefinitely. This directly contradicts the initial statement in Section 11, creating uncertainty about the return or destruction of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. The Recipient is not required to destroy any Confidential Information, regardless of a written request by the Disclosing Party. The Recipient may retain all documents indefinitely, irrespective of form or medium, including all Derivative Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 directly contradicts the original agreement by explicitly stating that the Recipient is not required to destroy any confidential information, regardless of a written request. This directly opposes the initial obligation, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, highlighting the direct opposition. The explanation underscores that this creates uncertainty about the return or destruction of confidential information. This meets the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction as it fundamentally alters the obligations of the Recipient regarding confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement is distinct and severable and a declaration of invalidity or unenforceability of any provision by a court of competent jurisdiction will not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter. No amendment or waiver of this Agreement will be binding unless executed in writing by the party to be bound.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. None of the provisions contained in this Agreement are distinct and severable and a declaration of invalidity or unenforceability of any provision by a court of competent jurisdiction will affect the validity or enforceability of all other provisions. This Agreement does not constitute the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter. Any amendment or waiver of this Agreement will be binding, even if it is not executed in writing by the party to be bound.
perturbation.explanation: The original text contains a severability clause, an integration clause, and requirements for written modifications. The changed text negates all three. Now, if any part of the agreement is invalid, the entire agreement is invalid. This contradicts the concept of severability. It also states the agreement isn't the entire agreement, and verbal amendments are binding, directly contradicting the original statement about written amendments being required. This change contradicts the original wording of Section 16.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. None of the provisions contained in this Agreement are distinct and severable and a declaration of invalidity or unenforceability of any provision by a court of competent jurisdiction will affect the validity or enforceability of all other provisions. This Agreement does not constitute the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter. Any amendment or waiver of this Agreement will be binding, even if it is not executed in writing by the party to be bound.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 16 directly contradicts the original intent of the section, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The original Section 16 contained a severability clause, an integration clause, and a requirement for written modifications. The changed text negates all three, stating that the agreement isn't the entire agreement, and verbal amendments are binding, and that the agreement is not severable. Therefore, it contradicts the purpose of the original Section 16.

perturbation.original_text: 19. Each of the parties hereto expressly agree that they will not, and will not permit any of its Representatives to directly or indirectly, hire an employee of the other party, or solicit or induce any employee of the other party to leave such employee’s position with the other party, for a period of one year. However, this paragraph shall not prohibit the Potential Acquiror or any of its affiliates from soliciting or hiring an employee who responds to a general solicitation of employment.
perturbation.changed_text: 19. Each of the parties hereto expressly agree that they can, and will permit any of its Representatives to directly or indirectly, hire an employee of the other party, or solicit or induce any employee of the other party to leave such employee’s position with the other party, for a period of any amount of time. However, this paragraph shall prohibit the Potential Acquiror or any of its affiliates from soliciting or hiring an employee who responds to a general solicitation of employment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text contains a no-hire agreement, restricting hiring or solicitation of employees for one year. The changed text completely reverses this, stating the parties *can* hire each other's employees and solicit them to leave. This contradicts the initial statement of Section 19 and creates legal uncertainty. Also, the second part of the sentence has been contradicted, since it now prohibits from hiring an employee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. Each of the parties hereto expressly agree that they can, and will permit any of its Representatives to directly or indirectly, hire an employee of the other party, or solicit or induce any employee of the other party to leave such employee’s position with the other party, for a period of any amount of time. However, this paragraph shall prohibit the Potential Acquiror or any of its affiliates from soliciting or hiring an employee who responds to a general solicitation of employment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 19 directly contradicts the original meaning of the section, which restricts hiring for one year. The changed text states the parties *can* hire each other's employees for *any amount of time*, a complete reversal. The second part of the sentence has also been contradicted, since it now *prohibits* from hiring an employee. Because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, it still creates an in-text contradiction. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction as defined by structural flaws.

perturbation.original_text: 9. All right, title and interest in and to a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information will remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and the Confidential Information will be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and its Representatives. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out in this Agreement, is granted to the Recipient under this Agreement by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. All right, title and interest in and to a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information will transfer to the Recipient upon disclosure. The Confidential Information will not be held in trust or confidence by the Recipient and its Representatives. Full rights, title, and interest respecting the Confidential Information is granted to the Recipient under this Agreement by implication and otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the disclosing party retains all rights to the confidential information, and the recipient holds it in confidence. The modified clause states that all rights transfer to the recipient, and the recipient is not obligated to keep it confidential. This reversal directly contradicts the initial statement and the core intent of the NDA. The entirety of the sentence in section 9 is being directly contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. All right, title and interest in and to a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information will transfer to the Recipient upon disclosure. The Confidential Information will not be held in trust or confidence by the Recipient and its Representatives. Full rights, title, and interest respecting the Confidential Information is granted to the Recipient under this Agreement by implication and otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 9 directly contradicts itself. It initially states that all rights, title, and interest transfer to the recipient upon disclosure. Subsequently, it asserts that the confidential information will not be held in trust or confidence. Finally, it provides the recipient with full rights, title, and interest under the agreement. This statement directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text since it reverses the confidentiality obligations and ownership of the information. The change creates a clear and direct conflict within the document regarding the core principle of an NDA: the protection of confidential information. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
